cause: Accessibility text bounds, visual OCR ROI results, overlay retention, and YouTube scroll/mini-player lifecycle are not yet sharing one strict validity window.
change: Recorded as a separate failure before further Android masking changes so later commits can be judged against this exact symptom.
problem: User screenshot shows masks remaining on profile/channel metadata and thumbnail regions while visible harmful text can still remain after scrolling; the mini-player also overlaps the feed, increasing stale overlay risk.
remaining_risk: Need next Android loop to invalidate visual masks on active scroll/miniplayer transitions and only render masks tied to current viewport generation.
title: YouTube scroll and mini-player leave stale masks on wrong regions
verification: Manual evidence from 2026-05-17 user screenshot in Codex session 019e3546-cfae-7450-880d-006c2f1102d5; no code fix claimed by this entry.

cause: MaskOverlayController.translateBy returned the failed translation status before clearing active views, and the service marked overlay revision stale while preserving masks for unresolved scroll deltas.
change: translateBy now clears active overlay views when translation produces no specs; unresolved or unrenderable scroll translations clear the overlay immediately and schedule recapture.
problem: Scroll translation could return ALL_OFFSCREEN, NO_TRANSLATABLE_MASKS, or REJECTED_DELTA without removing the existing overlay views, leaving stale masks visible until a later recapture.
remaining_risk: This reduces stale wrong-position masks, but first-frame exposure and OCR latency still need runtime validation on an attached emulator/device.
title: Clear untranslatable masks during YouTube scroll recapture gaps
verification: Focused MaskOverlayPlannerTest/MaskOverlayEventPolicyTest passed; :app:testDebugUnitTest passed; :app:assembleDebug passed; git diff --check passed. No device was attached for APK install.
